Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Dismiss, Or Alternatively, Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jun-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Law and Motion 302, Defendant's (John Stewart Company, Inc.) motion to compel arbitration of Plaintiff's (Maximillian Jones) individual PAGA claims.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The court adopts the following tentative ruling:

Defendant's (John Stewart Company, Inc.) motion to compel arbitration of Plaintiff's (Maximillian Jones) individual PAGA claims is granted. Plaintiff's representative PAGA claims are ordered stayed pending arbitration of the individual PAGA claims. (9 U.S.C. sec. 3.) The Federal Arbitration Act (FAA) makes agreements to arbitrate "valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract." (AT&T Mobility LLC v. Concepcion (2011) 563 U.S. 333, 336; 9 U.S.C. sec. 2.)
The party seeking arbitration bears the burden of proving the existence of an arbitration agreement, and the party opposing arbitration bears the burden of proving any defense. (Pinnacle Museum Tower Assn. v. Pinnacle Market Development (US), LLC. (2012) 55 Cal.4th 223, 236.) Defendant provided a copy of the arbitration agreement signed by Plaintiff. (See Choi Decl., Ex. B.) Plaintiff does not dispute the authenticity of this agreement. (Opp. at 5:14.) The agreement is governed by the FAA and contains a delegation clause. (Choi Decl., Ex. B at 1.) The delegation clause, which states that "the Arbitrator, and not any court, shall have exclusive authority to resolve any dispute relating to the validity, applicability, enforceability, unconscionability or waiver of this Agreement", is clear and unmistakable. (Id.) Plaintiff does not argue that the delegation clause is revocable under state contract defenses. The delegation clause is enforceable. (Pinela v. Neiman Marcus Group, Inc. (2015) 238 Cal.App.4th 227, 239-40.) Thus, it is for the arbitrator, not the court, to decide whether Plaintiff's individual PAGA claims are subject to the arbitration agreement. (See Tiri v. Lucky Chances, Inc. (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 231, 250.) The court declines to follow the non-binding statutory interpretation of state law regarding PAGA standing from Viking River Cruises v. Moriana (2022) 142 S.Ct. 1906. Plaintiff's representative PAGA claims are ordered stayed pending arbitration of the individual PAGA claims. (9 U.S.C. sec. 3.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU) ****After hearing: an order is signed. (See scanned order for details)
